oauth_app_analysis.context: Frequently appears in AI attack alert payloads as OAuthObjectId across multiple users (user2, user3, user4, user5, user6, user1).
oauth_app_analysis.interpretation: No AuditLogs records referencing this object ID as InitiatedBy, TargetResources, or AdditionalDetails. This strongly suggests the object ID is NOT a registered Entra application — it is likely an Azure OpenAI resource identifier or an API key handle rather than a consented OAuth app. Azure AI Content Safety / Prompt Shields surface this ID because it represents the caller of the AI endpoint (e.g., <AzureOpenAIResource> deployment). Recommend Azure Resource Graph / Cognitive Services REST lookup to confirm.

correlation_findings: user1's Entra sign-ins (198.51.100.40) do NOT intersect with any AI-attack source IP (203.0.113.10 on Apr 17 — 0 sign-ins). The AI API traffic bypassed Entra — possible stolen API key or exfiltrated session token.

user1 is part of a 6+ user cluster exhibiting AI attack behavior sharing the same OAuth object ID — indicates a systemic abuse pattern against a specific Azure OpenAI / Foundry deployment, not isolated compromise.
3 consecutive days (Apr 16, 18, 21) of HIGH-severity Purview IRM alerts on user1 — trending escalation pattern.
Content-filter gap previously observed (alert #43139 on <AzureOpenAIResource> detected-but-not-blocked) — indicates Prompt Shields action configuration is detect-only for some paths.
DLP violations on Apr 14 + Apr 16 (OneDrive document sharing) correlate temporally with the Apr 15 and Apr 17 AI attack clusters — possible pre-staging of data before AI exfiltration attempts.

immediate: Rotate any Azure OpenAI / Cognitive Services API keys accessible to user1.
Review all sign-ins for user1 on 2026-04-20 (138 sign-ins, ~75% failure ratio) — possible account spraying against admin account.
Escalate Insider Risk case — 3 consecutive HIGH-sev IRM alerts with supporting DLP violations.
Confirm whether Prompt Shields is in Block mode (not Detect-only) on the Foundry deployment(s) flagged by these alerts.
short_term: Conduct user interview — AI tool usage pattern (legitimate security research vs. policy violation).
Enforce Conditional Access policy requiring compliant device + phishing-resistant MFA for user1.
Azure Resource Graph lookup on OAuth object ID <OAuthObjectId> to identify owning resource.
Enrich other user accounts in the cluster (user2, user3, user4, user5, user6) for joint investigation.
strategic: Deploy Microsoft Purview DSPM for AI continuous monitoring across all Foundry / Azure OpenAI deployments.
Implement network-layer IP allowlisting for AI endpoints (block residential ISPs like Comcast AS7922 from API calls).

methodology.fallback_strategy: If query_lake MCP fails: fall back to Sentinel Data Lake KQL REST API (api.securityplatform.microsoft.com) or Microsoft Graph runHuntingQuery. Shodan primary API → InternetDB automatic fallback in enrich_ips.py.
assumptions_and_gaps: Sign-in data for Apr 20 (138 sign-ins, ~75% fail) was observed in triage but not fully drilled into — recommend separate investigation.
OAuth object ID <OAuthObjectId> was not resolved to its underlying resource — requires Azure Resource Graph / Microsoft Graph /applications lookup.
SessionId-based auth tracing not performed (no SessionId was surfaced in the Suspicious API Traffic alert payload reviewed).
